extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won a two-up drag race against Colbrelli on the 3.2km finishing climb, repeating his 2019 victory on the identical finish
tactical_patterns: Multiple breakaway reformations indicate headwind neutralized early moves; teams conserving for final climb despite lumpy profile.
Jumbo-Visma control from 7.7km despite no sprinter suggests wind-splitting or GC positioning as primary objective.
Wet technical descent 26km out caused selection more than final climb; Carapaz crashed showing GC riders vulnerable descending.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Executed exact same finish as 2019, suggesting pre-race reconnaissance and specific preparation for this gradient
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: negative: Lost two-up sprint despite European champion status; required medical assistance post-finish indicating bronchitis still impacting
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Crashed on wet descent 26km out requiring Ineos relay back; technical descending vulnerability exposed
rider_signals.PETERS Nans: negative: Bridged to break then immediately punctured with no team car support revealing poor gap management
